sections.paragraph: the tenth Hebrew month; from the new moon in January to that in February, Est 2:16. “The tenth month which is called by the Hebrews Tebeth, and by the Egyptians Τύβι (in la Croze Τώβι; in Cod. Vienn. Τήβι. Arabic طوبة), by the Romans January.” Jerome, on Eze 39:1. But the Egyptian month now mentioned, extended from the 20th of December, to the 20th of January.
